sources.reasoning: The agent's answer asserts many specific requirements but fails to cite any explicit CAISO documents (BPM names/sections, Tariff citations, publication dates or URLs). Expected authoritative sources for this question are the CAISO Business Practice Manual(s) for Market Operations and Ancillary Services (specific BPM section names/numbers and revision date), the CAISO Tariff (relevant sections), and CAISO published certification/testing guides or Compliance Monitoring BPM. Without explicit citations to those documents the claims cannot be verified. Several specific items are suspicious or inconsistent with CAISO practice and therefore require citation: the stated minimum capacity thresholds (500 kW general minimum; 100 kW for storage/DER aggregation), the numeric performance threshold stated as “25%” (CAISO uses specific performance metrics such as Regulation Performance scores and may use different thresholds or scoring methods), precise telemetry/AGC requirements (DNP3, exact telemetry points, UCON/UCTL naming), response times (10 minutes to reach maximum regulation), and the detailed financial/net-worth figures and bidding deadlines. The answer also mixes implementation-level details (e.g., DNP3) with high-level market rules without source attribution. Appropriate evaluation would cite the exact CAISO BPM (title and section), the CAISO Tariff sections referenced, and any CAISO notices or BPM revision history (e.g., revision date) that support claims. Because the answer includes many plausible-sounding statements but lacks authoritative citations and contains several likely inaccuracies/overgeneralizations, I rate the source validity low (2) and recommend the agent supply exact CAISO document references (BPM titles/sections, Tariff sections) or direct CAISO links for each substantive claim.

attribute_details.name: Registration and contracting
attribute_details.expected: Resource must be fully registered and contracted: complete the New Resource Implementation (NRI) process, have a Scheduling Coordinator (SC), and execute required agreements (e.g., Participating Generator Agreement).
attribute_details.agent_value: Mentions requirement for a certified Scheduling Coordinator, certification, master file records, and certification/testing before providing services; includes Scheduling Coordinator financial/credit requirements. Does not state the New Resource Implementation (NRI) process or explicitly reference execution of required agreements (e.g., Participating Generator Agreement).

attribute_details.name: Metering and telemetry standards
attribute_details.expected: Install ISO‑certified revenue‑quality meters and provide direct real‑time telemetry to CAISO's EMS for fast response and accurate control.
attribute_details.agent_value: Specifies real-time DNP3 telemetry datapoints, direct monitoring by CAISO EMS, bumpless AGC transfer, and telemetry fields (UCON, UCTL, AGC status). Does not mention ISO-certified revenue-quality meters or explicit revenue metering certification language.

attribute_details.name: Ancillary‑service technical certification and performance
attribute_details.expected: Obtain ancillary service (regulation) certification including documented control/protection settings and completed Generator Resource Data Template (GRDT); demonstrate ability to follow Dispatch Operating Point (DOP) and meet CAISO regulation performance standards.
attribute_details.agent_value: Describes CAISO certification and testing, references tariff sections, testing to adjust output within 10 minutes, required AGC/UAGC/UCON/UCTL capabilities, performance monitoring (rolling averages, Regulation Up/Down), and decertification process. Does not explicitly reference GRDT, documented control/protection settings, or explicit mention of following a Dispatch Operating Point (DOP).

attribute_details.name: Resource‑type specific requirements
attribute_details.expected: Comply with resource‑type rules: for storage maintain sufficient state of charge to sustain awarded regulation (e.g., 30 minutes at awarded level); for demand response/PDRs/DERs meet Net Benefits Test, Sub‑LAP mapping, proper resource IDs and aggregation structures as defined in the BPM.
attribute_details.agent_value: Includes resource-specific notes: storage and DER aggregations minimum 100 kW, aggregation participation allowed, and general statement that storage/DERs must meet technical and performance requirements. States dispatchability durations (60 minutes DAM, 30 minutes RTM) but does not explicitly state storage must maintain state-of-charge for 30 minutes at awarded level, nor mention Net Benefits Test, Sub-LAP mapping, resource IDs, or specific aggregation structure requirements.
